title: Student navigation, Mastery Goals, and assignments
description: Strategies for implementing course mastery with students
topic_path: Supporting student progress with Khan Academy
Student navigation, mastery goals, and assignments
Student navigation, Mastery Goals, and assignments

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank.**

When assigning mastery of a course, students will have to master all skills in the course to reach $100\%$. Realistically, we recommend students aim to master about  [[☃ dropdown 1]] of the course.
hints: When assigning mastery of a course, students will have to master all skills in the course to reach $100\%$. Realistically, we recommend students aim to master about  $80\%$ of the course.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What should I encourage students to do when they get stuck? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Fill in the blank.**

To make sure students stay on track with their mastery goal, encourage them to look for the [[☃ dropdown 1]] buttons.
hints: To make sure students stay on track with their mastery goal, encourage them to look for the **blue** buttons.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the recommended first step to using Khan Academy with students?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Setting a Course mastery goal is a recommended first step to using Khan Academy with students. Course mastery goals allow students to have a clear, long-term focus on what they want to achieve throughout the year.

assessment_items.question_content:
Students should play an active role in the goal-setting and tracking process. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**On Khan Academy, where can students view their mastery goal progress?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Students can always view their Course mastery goals and progress from their **Learner home**.

options.alt: A Khan Academy page. At the top, it says my mastery goals. Below that are all the goals listed for the student.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blanks.**

It is recommended that teachers work with students to set short-term [[☃ dropdown 1]] mastery goals and long-term [[☃ dropdown 2]] mastery goals.
hints: It is recommended that teachers work with students to set short-term **unit** mastery goals and long-term **course** mastery goals.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a Khan Academy landing page.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the name of this screen?**

[[☃ radio 3]]
hints: This is the **Learner home**, which is what students see when they log into Khan Academy.

options.alt: A Khan Academy page. At the top, it says the name of a student. Below is a heading that says my courses. Then below that, it shows the courses the student is enrolled in.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is an image of a Khan Academy landing page.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Fill in the blank.**

Students will see current assignments in the order of what is due [[☃ dropdown 2]] at the top of the list.
hints: Students will see current assignments in the order of what is due **soonest** at the top of the list

options.alt: A Khan Academy page. At the top is the name of a student. Below that is a heading that say my assignments. Below that is all the assignments, their due dates, and the status.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is an image of a Khan Academy landing page.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Fill in the blanks.**

When students click on their mastery goal they are taken to the [[☃ dropdown 3]] page where they can work through the course or unit at [[☃ dropdown 4]] pace.
hints: When students click on their mastery goal they are taken to the **course or unit** page where they can work through the course or unit at **their own** pace.

options.alt: A Khan Academy page. At the top, it says my mastery goals. Below that are all the goals listed for the student.

assessment_items.question_content:
Parents/guardians can be essential allies in supporting students in their mastery learning journey. Having a positive supporter at home who recognizes the student’s effort, persistence and achievement can be influential on a student’s success.

**True of false.**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**What should I encourage students to do when they get stuck?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**As a student, any past or overdue assignments can be found in the Past tab. **

[[☃ radio 1]]
